title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Do animals perceive time differently from humans?
items.description: <cite>Science</cite> chats with a researcher whose team is using “timescapes” to understand how nonhumans experience the world

items.title: A space telescope is falling to Earth. NASA is racing to rescue it
items.description: Vehicle will attempt a daring capture-and-boost mission to extend the life of the Swift observatory

items.title: Big Ebola outbreak puts spotlight on little-known virus
items.description: The Bundibugyo virus only emerged twice before. Now, scientists see a chance to get to know it better

items.title: The earliest plague epidemics may have been caused by marmots
items.description: Ancient hunter-gatherers may have contracted the pathogen from marmots

items.title: New space telescope will map galaxies’ ghostly halos and streams
items.description: European Space Agency mission will trace the relics of ancient mergers and probe the dark matter shaping galaxy growth

items.title: Russia’s plan to drill superdeep holes in Arctic revives controversial theory of ‘endless oil’
items.description: Soviet-era theory touted by Putin’s former campaign manager claims oil deposits can form without organic matter

items.title: Dismantling of key ocean ‘telescope’ raises fears of U.S. retreat from marine science
items.description: Researchers are pushing back on abrupt NSF cuts to the costly Ocean Observatories Initiative

items.title: AI models have a troubling knack for discovering legal loopholes
items.description: AIs on their own found ways to exploit regulations and evade current safeguards

items.title: French physicist and media star loses doctorate after plagiarism investigation
items.description: University punishes Étienne Klein after investigation shows he plagiarized part of his Ph.D. thesis

items.title: New documentary follows researchers’ increasingly fraught career path
items.description: The Endless Frontier traces the lives of three scientists as they train students and deal with funding pressures
